ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'doughnut' class (FOODON_03301713). No specific 'chocolate cream-filled doughnut' subclass exists; closest_parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers doughnuts generically. No filled-chocolate-cream variant subcode exists; closest_parent defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for chocolate cream-filled doughnut identified in published SNOMED CT food hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate volcano donut weighing approximately 90 g, as typically served at Japanese-style bakeries in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Doughnuts, cake-type, chocolate-coated or frosted (FDC ID 172757) and Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, with creme filling (FDC ID 172756)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for chocolate-type doughnuts and cream-filled yeast doughnuts. The input record's per_100g values (energy 388.89 kcal, protein 6.11 g, fat 22.22 g, carbohydrate 42.22 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for cream-filled chocolate doughnuts and are retained. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several micronutrients not reported in the input are set to -1 where USDA data for this specific filled variant is not directly available at the required granularity.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Doughnut, cake type (GI=76, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 76 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables for cake-type doughnut (glucose reference = 100). The Chocolate Volcano Donut is a filled, baked doughnut with a refined wheat flour base and high sugar content; the cake-type doughnut GI is the closest defensible analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 42.22 × 0.90) / 100 = (76 × 38.0) / 100 ≈ 28.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (22.2 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.67 g/100g), and elevated saturated fat (11.11 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this donut entirely: sugar at 22.2 g/100g with fibre below 2 g/100g triggers the solid-food sugar gate, and saturated fat at 11.11 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g cardiovascular threshold, compounding arterial stiffness risk independent of its moderate sodium content (200 mg/100g).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 22.2 g/100g exceeds the 20 g/100g hard gate; high glycemic index (76) and glycemic load per serving (~28.8) from refined wheat flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: This donut is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes: sugar at 22.2 g/100g exceeds the 20 g/100g disqualifying threshold, the high GI of 76 and glycemic load of ~28.8 per serving will produce a rapid and large postprandial glucose spike, and the low dietary fibre (1.67 g/100g) provides no meaningful glycaemic buffering.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 11.11 g/100g, trans fat at 0.56 g/100g, cholesterol at 50 mg/100g, and high sugar load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this donut: saturated fat at 11.11 g/100g and trans fat at 0.56 g/100g raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis, cholesterol at 50 mg/100g compounds cardiovascular risk, and the high sugar content (22.2 g/100g) independently elevates triglycerides and cardiovascular event risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chocolate Volcano Donut is a Japanese-style baked doughnut sold at specialty bakeries in Singapore, characterised by a lightly crisp exterior, a soft and airy dough shell, and a generous chocolate crème filling that oozes when bitten into — evoking the 'volcano' name. The dough is made from refined wheat flour, eggs, butter, milk, and sugar, then baked rather than deep-fried, and finished with a dusting of sugar sprinkles on top. Per 100 g it delivers approximately 389 kcal, 6.1 g protein, 22.2 g fat (of which 11.1 g is saturated fat and 0.56 g is trans fat), 42.2 g carbohydrate, and 22.2 g sugar, with only 1.67 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 200 mg/100g and cholesterol is 50 mg/100g. The glycemic index is approximately 76 (high), yielding a glycemic load of roughly 28.8 per standard 90 g serving. Clinically, this item is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (sugar exceeds 20 g/100g hard gate, high GI), hypertension (high sugar with low fibre, high saturated fat), or cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol all elevated). It is vegetarian but not vegan, contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, and is classified as ultra-processed. It is commonly consumed as a dessert or snack and is associated with Japanese-style bakery culture in Southeast Asia.
common_user_queries: Chocolate cream filled donut sugar content
Is chocolate donut suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values from the input record are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cream-filled chocolate-type doughnuts and have been retained as adapted estimates. No exact USDA entry for 'Chocolate Volcano Donut' exists; values are cross-validated against USDA FDC IDs 172756 and 172757. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cake-type doughnut. Confidence is medium because no exact approved-source match exists for this specific branded product.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific filled chocolate doughnut variant is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy at the required granularity as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the input record and are not available with sufficient specificity for this filled chocolate doughnut variant in USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. All core macros (protein_g 6.11, fat_g 22.22, carbohydrate_g 42.22) are non-zero and consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for cream-filled doughnuts; no zero-macro justification is required. The input record's cultural classification as 'Japanese' cuisine is retained as this product is a Japanese-style bakery item sold in Singapore; the country_of_origin is Singapore. Halal status is not confirmed and is set to false pending certification verification. The input listed 'diabetes' and 'hypertension' conditions; these have been mapped to 'Type 2 Diabetes' and 'Hypertension' per Veritas naming rules, and 'Cardiovascular Disease' has been added as a mandatory third condition.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type to 'closest_parent' as per G5 hard gate requirements.

Added mandatory local names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to local_names and aliases_by_language, and ensured they are present in the flat aliases array, addressing G6 and Q1/Q8 failures. The cuisine is Japanese, but the country_of_origin is Singapore, which triggers the Singaporean multilingual rules.
